📖  Grammar — Past Simple — storytelling with sequence words
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

In storytelling, we use Past Simple with SEQUENCE WORDS to connect events and make stories flow naturally. Sequence words: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Next (дараачийн), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). These words tell the listener WHEN and IN WHAT ORDER things happened.

Түүх ярихдаа Past Simple-г ДАРААЛАЛЫН ҮГС-тэй хослуулдаг. Дараалалын үгс: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). Эдгээр үгс үйл явдлуудын ДАРААЛАЛыг харуулдаг.
At first I didn't like it, but THEN I loved it!
Дараалалын үгсийг хэрэглэж баян хэлж сур!
First... Then... After that... Finally... = гайхалтай түүхийн бүтэц
At first I DIDN'T understand. I WASN'T good at English. We DIDN'T have a good teacher.
WHEN did you start school?  WHERE did you grow up?  WHAT happened next?  HOW did you feel?
• First / Then / After that / Finally = sequence!
• At first... but then... = contrast and change
• When I was + age = talking about past events
❌ First I go to school.  ✅ First I WENT to school.
❌ Then suddenly I am happy.  ✅ Then suddenly I WAS happy.
❌ After that I move.  ✅ After that I MOVED.
❌ Finally I pass.  ✅ Finally I PASSED.

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect — have/has been (life experience)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) describes LIFE EXPERIENCES — things you have done at some unspecified time in your life, without saying exactly WHEN. Compare: 'I have been to Seoul.' (experience, no specific time) vs 'I went to Seoul last year.' (specific time = use Past Simple!). Signal words: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, before, once, twice.

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect — have/has + past participle (eaten/seen/met/done)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

Present Perfect uses HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (the 3rd form of the verb). Regular past participles add -ed (studied, visited, cooked). Irregular past participles must be memorized: eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten, win→won. This describes experiences without saying the exact time.

Present Perfect нь HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (3-р хэлбэр) ашигладаг. Ердийн үйл үгс: -ed нэм (studied, visited). Тэгдэггүй үйл үгс: цээжлэх ёстой. eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten. 📖  Grammar — Present Perfect with for and since — talking about duration
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

Use FOR with a PERIOD OF TIME (how long): for 3 years, for a month, for ages, for a long time. Use SINCE with a POINT IN TIME (start): since 2010, since Monday, since I was born, since last year. These go with Present Perfect when the situation continues to NOW: 'I have studied English for 30 days.' (started 30 days ago, still true now!)

FOR нь ХУГАЦААНЫ УРТЫГ (хэр удаан) илэрхийлдэг: for 3 years, for a month, for ages. SINCE нь ЭХЛЭХ ЦЭГийг илэрхийлдэг: since 2010, since Monday, since last year. Эдгээрийг Present Perfect-тэй хэрэглэдэг — нөхцөл байдал ОДОО хүртэл үргэлжилж байгааг харуулна.
I have studied for a month.
I haven't studied for a week.
Since when have you lived here?
How long have you lived here?
I have known him for ages.
How long have you known him?
I have loved it since childhood.
I've studied FOR a month · She's lived here SINCE 2010
How long HAVE you studied? · How long HAS she worked?
FOR = хугацааны урт (for 3 years) · SINCE = эхлэх цэг (since 2010)
I HAVEN'T studied for a week. She HASN'T lived here since 2020. They HAVEN'T spoken for ages.
HOW LONG have you studied?  SINCE WHEN have you lived here?  HOW LONG has she worked there?
• FOR = length of time: for 3 years, for a month
• SINCE = starting point: since 2010, since Monday
• Quick test: 'For how long?' → FOR  'Starting when?' → SINCE

❌ I have studied since 3 months.  ✅ I have studied FOR 3 months.
❌ She lives here since 2010.  ✅ She HAS LIVED here since 2010.
❌ I know him for years.  ✅ I HAVE KNOWN him for years.

KEY RULE: Use PRESENT PERFECT when there is NO specific time (experience, recent events, continuing situations). Use PAST SIMPLE when there IS a specific time (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). Signal words for Present Perfect: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PRESENT PERFECT — тодорхой цаг БАЙХГҮЙ үед (туршлага, саяхан болсон зүйл, үргэлжилж буй нөхцөл байдал). PAST SIMPLE — тодорхой цаг БАЙГАА үед (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). PP дохио үгс: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. PS дохио үгс: yesterday, last week, ago, in [year].

📖  Grammar — Too / Enough — expressing excess and sufficiency
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

TOO means MORE THAN NEEDED (negative meaning, there is a problem): too hot, too tired, too expensive. It comes BEFORE adjectives. ENOUGH means THE RIGHT AMOUNT (neutral or positive): big enough, fast enough. It comes AFTER adjectives. NOT ENOUGH means LESS THAN NEEDED: not big enough, not fast enough. Pattern: too + adj + to + verb → 'The soup is too hot TO EAT.'

TOO нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНЭЭС ИХ (сөрөг утга, асуудал байгаа) гэсэн утгатай: too hot, too tired, too expensive. Тэмдэг нэрийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг. ENOUGH нь ЗОХИСТОЙ ХЭМЖЭЭг (саармаг эсвэл эерэг) илэрхийлдэг. Тэмдэг нэрийн ДАРАА ирдэг. NOT ENOUGH нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНД ХҮРЭХГҮЙ: not big enough. Хэлбэр: too + тэмдэг нэр + to + үйлүг.
not + adjective + enough = not big enough
TOO — тэмдэг нэрийн ӨМНӨ · ENOUGH — тэмдэг нэрийн ДАРАА

Is it TOO HOT? Is it BIG ENOUGH? Is there ENOUGH time? Are there TOO MANY people?
• TOO = problem! Too hot to eat. Too tired to work.
• ENOUGH = just right. Big enough. Fast enough.

WOULD RATHER expresses preference between two options: 'I would rather STAY home than GO out.' = I prefer staying home. After 'would rather': BARE INFINITIVE (no 'to'). Ихэнх тэмдэг нэрт -LY нэмэхэд тодотгол үг болдог: quick→quickly · careful→carefully · confident→confidently.
📖  Grammar — Adverbs — quickly, carefully, well, badly, hard
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

Adverbs describe HOW an action is done. Most adverbs are formed by adding -LY to adjectives: quick→quickly, careful→carefully, slow→slowly. IRREGULAR adverbs: good→well, fast→fast (same!), hard→hard (same!), early→early (same!). Position: adverbs usually come AFTER the verb or AFTER the object: 'She speaks English WELL.' 'He drives CAREFULLY.' Never between verb and object!

Тодотгол үгс нь үйлдлийг ЯАГАН хийснийг тайлбарладаг. Ихэнх тодотгол үгс тэмдэг нэрт -LY нэмж үүсдэг: quick→quickly, careful→carefully. ДУР МЭДЭН хэлбэр: good→well, fast→fast (өөрчлөгдөхгүй!), hard→hard. Байрлал: тодотгол үг ихэвчлэн үйл үгийн ДАРАА эсвэл объектын ДАРАА ирдэг.
She is careful → She drives carefully.
He is good at English → He speaks well.
She is fast → She runs fast.
How often does she work hard?
Тэмдэг нэр → Тодотгол үг: careful→carefully (-ly нэмдэг)

HOW does she speak? (She speaks fluently.) HOW did he drive? (Carefully.) HOW hard do you work?
• Adverbs describe HOW → come after the verb
❌ She speaks English good.  ✅ She speaks English WELL.
❌ He works hardly.  ✅ He works HARD. (hard is already an adverb)
❌ She quickly speaks.  ✅ She speaks QUICKLY. (after the verb)
❌ He drives careful.  ✅ He drives CAREFULLY.

Opinion — 3 үе: uh-PIN-yun. However — 3 үе: how-EV-er. Although — 'th' нь voiced (the-тэй адил).
📖  Grammar — I think / I believe / In my opinion — expressing and defending views
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

To express opinions in English, use phrases like: 'I think...', 'I believe...', 'In my opinion...', 'Personally...', 'I feel...'. To AGREE: 'I agree!', 'That's a good point.', 'Absolutely!', 'Exactly!'. To DISAGREE politely: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...', 'Actually, I think...'. Always give a REASON after your opinion using 'because', 'for example', 'such as'.

ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХГҮЙ ЗОХИСТОЙГООР: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...'. Санал бодлынхоо дараа ШАЛТГААН заавал нэм — 'because', 'for example'.

I think + reason (because) · I believe + evidence · In my opinion + argument

Бүх reflexive pronoun-ийн хүчлэл: my-SELF, your-SELF, him-SELF, her-SELF, our-SELVES, them-SELVES.
📖  Grammar — Reflexive pronouns — myself, yourself, himself, herself, ourselves, themselves
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

Reflexive pronouns are used when the SUBJECT and OBJECT are the SAME person. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (I hurt me — same person). Also used for EMPHASIS: 'I made this MYSELF.' (nobody helped me, I did it alone — this stresses independence). Forms: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Common phrases: by myself (alone), help yourself (take what you want), enjoy yourself (have a good time).

Reflexive pronoun нь субьект ба объект ИЖИЛ хүн байх үед хэрэглэгддэг. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (Би өөртөө гэмтлээ). Мөн ОНЦОЛЖ хэлэхэд хэрэглэнэ: 'I made this MYSELF.' (хэн ч тусалсангүй). Хэлбэрүүд: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Нийтлэг хэллэгүүд: by myself (ганцаараа), help yourself (авч ид), enjoy yourself (хөгжилдөх).
by myself = alone · help yourself = please take some
enjoy yourself = have fun · be yourself = act naturally

enjoy yourself — сайхан хөгжил. behave yourself — зөв ав.

Did you do it YOURSELF? Can he fix it HIMSELF? Did they enjoy THEMSELVES? Are you by YOURSELF?

• by myself / by yourself / by himself = alone

📖  Grammar — Should / Shouldn't — giving advice and recommendations
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

SHOULD expresses ADVICE, RECOMMENDATIONS, and what is the right thing to do. It is LESS STRONG than 'must' or 'have to'. Form: subject + SHOULD + BASE VERB (no 'to'!). 📖  Grammar — Some / Any / A lot of — countable and uncountable quantities
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

SOME is used in POSITIVE sentences and OFFERS/REQUESTS: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY is used in NEGATIVE sentences and QUESTIONS: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF works with BOTH countable and uncountable nouns: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (countable) vs A LITTLE (uncountable): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.

SOME — ЭЕРЭГ өгүүлбэр болон санал/хүсэлтэд: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY — ҮГҮЙСГЭЛ ба АСУУЛТ: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF — хоёуланд нь: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (тоолж болох) vs A LITTLE (тоолшгүй): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.
I have a lot of eggs.
Do you have a lot of rice?
Do you have a few minutes?
Can you add a little milk?
a few → countable (eggs, friends) · a little → uncountable (milk, salt)
SOME = эерэг + санал · ANY = үгүйсгэл + асуулт
A FEW = тоолж болно (eggs) · A LITTLE = тоолшгүй (milk, water)

Do you have ANY eggs? Is there ANY milk? Would you like SOME tea? How MUCH rice is there?
• some = positive + offers: 'I have some eggs.'
• any = negative + questions: 'Do you have any?'
• a few + countable: a few eggs / a few friends
• a little + uncountable: a little milk / a little time

❌ I have a few milk.  ✅ I have A LITTLE milk. (uncountable!)
❌ Add a little eggs.  ✅ Add A FEW eggs. (countable!)

📖  Grammar — Too much / Too many / Not enough — quantity problems
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

TOO MUCH + uncountable noun (problem): 'There is too much noise.' TOO MANY + countable noun (problem): 'There are too many people.' NOT ENOUGH + noun (insufficient): 'I don't have enough money.' These three structures all express PROBLEMS with quantities. Compare: too much noise (you can't count noise) vs too many people (you can count people: 1 person, 2 people). NOT ENOUGH can follow with 'to + verb': 'Not enough money TO buy it.'

TOO MUCH + тоолшгүй нэр (асуудал): 'There is too much noise.' TOO MANY + тоолж болох нэр (асуудал): 'There are too many people.' NOT ENOUGH + нэр (хангалтгүй): 'I don't have enough money.' Is there TOO MUCH noise? Are there TOO MANY people? Do you have ENOUGH money? Is it TOO MUCH?
• too much + uncountable: too much noise, money, time
• too many + countable: too many people, clothes, bags
• not enough + any noun: not enough money/time/chairs
• Quick test: can you count it? → too many / too much

❌ Not enough of money.  ✅ Not enough money. (no 'of'!)
❌ There are too much clothes.  ✅ There are TOO MANY clothes.

The FIRST CONDITIONAL expresses REAL and POSSIBLE future situations. Structure: IF + Present Simple, WILL + base verb. The IF clause describes a possible condition; the WILL clause describes the likely result. 'If I save money, I will travel to Korea.' The IF clause can come first or second: 'I will travel to Korea IF I save money.' Both mean the same thing! Note: always use PRESENT SIMPLE after 'if' in First Conditional — NEVER 'will' after 'if'.

FIRST CONDITIONAL нь БОДИТ болон БОЛОМЖТОЙ ирээдүйн нөхцлийг илэрхийлдэг. Хэлбэр: IF + Present Simple, WILL + үндсэн үйл үг. IF хэсэг нь боломжтой нөхцлийг, WILL хэсэг нь тохиолдлын үр дүнг илэрхийлдэг. 'If I save money, I will travel to Korea.' АНХААРУУЛГА: 'if'-ийн дараа ХЭЗЭЭ Ч 'will' хэрэглэдэггүй — ҮРГЭЛЖ Present Simple!

If I don't save, I won't travel.

I won't = I will not · won't = the negative of will
I'll = I will (хэвийн яриандаа). Won't = will not.

• NEVER: 'If I will save' ❌ → 'If I save' ✅
• Unless = if not: 'Unless I save, I won't go'

📖  Grammar — Second Conditional — If I were rich, I would buy...
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The SECOND CONDITIONAL expresses UNREAL, IMAGINARY, or UNLIKELY situations in the present or future. Structure: IF + Past Simple, WOULD + base verb. 'If I were rich, I would travel the world.' (I am NOT rich — this is imaginary.) KEY: Use WERE for all persons with 'to be' (not 'was'): 'If I WERE you...' 'If she WERE here...' This is different from First Conditional (real/possible) → Second Conditional (unreal/imaginary).

SECOND CONDITIONAL нь БОДИТ БУС, ТӨСӨӨЛЛИЙН нөхцлийг илэрхийлдэг. Хэлбэр: IF + Past Simple, WOULD + үндсэн үйл үг. 'If I were rich, I would travel.' (Би баян биш — энэ бол төсөөлөл.) ЧУХАЛ: 'to be'-ийн хувьд бүх нэрт WERE хэрэглэнэ: 'If I WERE you...' 'If she WERE here...' First Conditional (бодит/боломжтой) ≠ Second Conditional (бодит бус/төсөөлөл).
If I were rich, I'd travel.
If I weren't rich, I wouldn't.
What would you do if you were rich?
If she had time, she'd help.
If she didn't have time, she wouldn't.
What would she do if she had time?
If they could fly, they'd go.
If they couldn't fly, they wouldn't.
What would they do if they could fly?
If I were you, I'd study more.
If I were you, I wouldn't worry.
I'd = I would · She'd = She would · He'd = He would
Wouldn't = would not · If I were you, I'd...
I'd = I would — хэвийн яриандаа хэрэглэ!
Wouldn't = would not. 'If I were you, I'd...' = маш нийтлэг зөвлөгөөний хэллэг.
If I WEREN'T busy, I WOULD help. If she DIDN'T have money, she WOULDN'T travel. I WOULDN'T worry.
What WOULD you do IF you were rich?  IF you could live anywhere, WHERE would you live?  WOULD you help IF you could?
• Use WERE (not was) with all subjects: 'If I were...'
• 'If I were you, I'd...' = giving advice politely

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

MUST expresses STRONG OBLIGATION or NECESSITY (often from the speaker or a rule): 'You must arrive on time.' MUSTN'T expresses PROHIBITION (something is not allowed): 'You mustn't be late.' HAVE TO also expresses obligation but comes from EXTERNAL rules or circumstances: 'I have to wear a uniform.' (company rule). DON'T HAVE TO means NOT NECESSARY (but you can if you want): 'You don't have to work weekends.' Key: MUSTN'T = prohibited (don't do it!) ≠ DON'T HAVE TO = not necessary (you can choose).

MUST нь ХҮЧТЭЙ ҮҮРЭГ эсвэл ЗАЙЛШГҮЙ байдлыг илэрхийлдэг (ихэвчлэн яригчаас эсвэл дүрмээс): 'You must arrive on time.' MUSTN'T нь ХОРИГЛОЛТЫГ илэрхийлдэг: 'You mustn't be late.' HAVE TO мөн үүргийг илэрхийлэх боловч ГАДНААС ирдэг: 'I have to wear a uniform.' (компанийн дүрэм). DON'T HAVE TO = ХИЙХ ШААРДЛАГАГҮЙ (хийж болно). ЧУХАЛ: MUSTN'T = ХОРИГЛОГДСОН (хийж болохгүй!) ≠ DON'T HAVE TO = шаардлагагүй.

📖  Grammar — Need to / Don't need to — necessity vs no necessity
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

NEED TO expresses NECESSITY — something that is required or important to do. It is similar to 'have to' but often refers to practical necessity. 'I need to finish this report.' (it is necessary for me to finish it). DON'T NEED TO means something is NOT NECESSARY — you have a choice. 'You don't need to come early.' (it's not required — but you can if you want). Negative of 'need to' ≠ mustn't! Don't need to = optional. Mustn't = prohibited.

NEED TO нь ЗАЙЛШГҮЙ БАЙДЛЫГ илэрхийлдэг — хийх шаардлагатай зүйл. 'I need to finish this report.' (дуусгах шаардлагатай). DON'T NEED TO нь ШААРДЛАГАГҮЙ гэсэн утгатай — чи сонгох эрхтэй. 'You don't need to come early.' (шаардлагагүй — гэхдээ хүсвэл ирж болно). DON'T NEED TO ≠ MUSTN'T! Don't need to = сонголттой. Mustn't = хориглогдсон.

WILL = 1) Тэр мөчид гарсан шийдвэр: 'I'll answer it!' 2) Нотолгоогүй таамаглал (бодол): 'I think English will be important.' 3) Амлалт ба санал.

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PASSIVE VOICE moves the focus from the DOER of the action to the RECEIVER. ACTIVE: 'Samsung makes this phone.' (focus on Samsung — the doer). PASSIVE: 'This phone is made by Samsung.' (focus on the phone — the receiver). PRESENT PASSIVE form: is/are + past participle. Use passive when: 1) The doer is unknown or unimportant. 2) The action is more important than who did it. 3) In formal/scientific writing. 'by + agent' is optional — often left out when unknown.

(утас дээр анхаарна). ОДОО ЦАГИЙН ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР: is/are + past participle. Хэзээ хэрэглэх: 1) Хийгч тодорхойгүй. 2) Үйл нь чухал. 3) Албан ёсны/шинжлэх ухааны бичиглэлд.

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PAST PASSIVE is formed with WAS/WERE + PAST PARTICIPLE. Use it to describe historical events and facts where the focus is on WHAT happened, not on WHO did it. WAS = singular subjects: 'The telephone WAS INVENTED by Bell.' WERE = plural subjects: 'Many cities WERE BUILT along the Silk Road.' Same rules as present passive: use 'by + agent' when the doer is important. Leave out 'by' when the doer is unknown or unimportant.

ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР: WAS/WERE + PAST PARTICIPLE. Түүхэн үйл явдал, баримтуудыг тайлбарлахад хэрэглэдэг — ХЭН хийснийг биш, ЮУ болсон гэдгийг онцолдог. WAS = нэгдмэл: 'The telephone WAS INVENTED.' WERE = олон тоо: 'Cities WERE BUILT.' 'by + agent' — хийгч чухал үед хэрэглэнэ. Тодорхойгүй үед 'by'-г орхиж болно.

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

REPORTED SPEECH (indirect speech) tells us what someone SAID without using their exact words. KEY CHANGES when reporting: 1) Verb tense shifts back: am→was, is→was, are→were, have→had, will→would, can→could. 2) Pronouns change: I→he/she, we→they, my→his/her. Direct: 'I am tired.' → Reported: She said (that) she WAS tired. Direct: 'I will help.' → Reported: He said he WOULD help. 'That' is optional after 'said' and 'told'.

📖  Grammar — Relative clauses — who, which, that
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

RELATIVE CLAUSES add extra information about a noun using WHO, WHICH, or THAT. WHO = for people: 'The student WHO studies hard will succeed.' WHICH = for things: 'The book WHICH I read was amazing.' THAT = for both people and things (informal): 'The film THAT we watched was great.' WHERE = for places: 'The library WHERE I study is quiet.' WHOSE = for possession: 'The artist WHOSE work I love is Sarnai.' Relative clauses join two sentences: 'I know a man. He speaks 5 languages.' → 'I know a man WHO speaks 5 languages.'

ХАРЬЦААНЫ ӨГҮҮЛБЭРИЙН ХЭСЭГ (relative clause) нь нэрийн тухай WHO, WHICH, THAT ашиглан нэмэлт мэдээлэл өгдөг. WHO = хүний тухай: 'The student WHO studies hard.' WHICH = зүйлийн тухай: 'The book WHICH I read.' THAT = хоёуланд (хэвийн ярианд): 'The film THAT we watched.' WHERE = газрын тухай: 'The library WHERE I study.' Relative clause нь хоёр өгүүлбэрийг нэгтгэдэг!
